text.title: gemini-api Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for gemini-api as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Accessing response.text when the response was safety-blocked.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A blocked response has no .text attribute; accessing it raises AttributeError. Always check response.prompt_feedback.block_reason is None before using response.text.

failure-mode.description: Using Gemini 3 with low temperature (e.g., 0.1) for structured extraction.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Gemini 3's dynamic thinking works best at default temperature 1.0. Low temperature suppresses reasoning. For structured output use response_mime_type="application/json" with response_schema instead of lowering temperature.

failure-mode.description: Uploading the same large file on every request instead of caching.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: File upload adds latency and does not benefit from context caching. Upload once, store the genai.File reference, reuse across sessions. Files are retained for 48 hours by default.

failure-mode.description: Using Gemini for tool-heavy agents without fallback to OpenAI.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Gemini function calling accuracy lags OpenAI GPT-4o on dense tool catalogues. For production agents with >10 tools, benchmark both providers before committing to Gemini.
